data.body:
RELATED ROBUSTNESS GAP (observed during the same incident): the review bot (src/rebar/review_bot/app.py) drains its in-memory asyncio.Queue with a SINGLE background worker. The per-event try/except only guards against exceptions — it does NOT guard against a HANG. When a review's clone/subprocess or LLM call blocks indefinitely (as happened when the disk was full mid-clone), that one worker is stuck forever on await, the queue silently backs up, and EVERY subsequent change (471, 472, plus other sessions' 461/462/474-478) gets no vote — no error, no log, just an inert bot accepting webhooks (202) and never processing. Recovery required a manual 'docker restart compose-review-bot-1' (which resets the worker + empties the in-memory queue) then re-/rerun of each change.
Suggested hardening (add to this bug's scope): (a) a per-review timeout (asyncio.wait_for around review_and_vote) so a hung review fails-closed to a coverage-gap vote instead of wedging the worker; (b) consider >1 worker or a watchdog; (c) the queue is in-memory, so a restart loses queued events — the backfill reconcile_loop should re-enqueue changes still lacking a current-patchset vote (verify it does).
